page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model with 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and 40 MB of L3 cache. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a performance profile characterized by average multi-core capabilities but weak single-core performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10 percent, and single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression are weak, scoring in the bottom 25 percent. However, multi-threaded performance is more robust, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and PassMark CPU scores all landing in the average category. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also demonstrate average performance, as does the PassMark memory score.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.e4.4xlarge is its reliance on older Intel Xeon architecture with shared CPU allocation, which limits single-core execution speed and makes it unsuitable for latency-sensitive or single-threaded workloads. For multi-threaded applications, the instance performs adequately, achieving average results in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. It is also capable of handling light LLM inference for small 135M models, though performance drops significantly on larger 7B models. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that require a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio but do not demand high-performance single-core compute, making it suitable for database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose multi-threaded backend tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.4xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate weak single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core scores in the bottom 10 percent. However, multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, multi-threaded workloads such as database caching, Redis deployments, and static web serving, where massive single-core compute power is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering average multi-core performance for database and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio, balanced multi-core performance, and shared Intel Xeon processors for database and caching workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
